message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts
Failing test: "dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive (reproduction)"
Current error: Test expects (1,1) in result given input [(0,0),(1,0),(0,1)]. Current impl only filters live cells; never adds dead cells.
Passing tests so far: 5

Implement the minimal code to make the failing test pass while keeping all other tests green.

The reproduction rule: any dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive in the next generation. Strategy: consider candidate cells (live cells + their neighbors), keep cells that have exactly 3 live neighbors (always alive next gen, whether previously alive or dead), or cells that are currently alive with exactly 2 live neighbors.

Do NOT refactor — that comes in the next phase.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal code to make the failing test pass. Let me start by reading the current state of both files.

Now I'll implement the minimal change. I need to consider candidate cells (live cells + their neighbors), and include cells with exactly 3 live neighbors (reproduction) or live cells with 2 live neighbors (survival).

content.text:
All 6 tests pass (with 2 todo remaining).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Generate candidate cells (all live cells + their neighbors), then filter to keep cells with exactly 3 live neighbors (reproduction) or live cells with exactly 2 live neighbors (survival).
- Result: All 6 tests pass (2 todo remaining)
- Approach: Minimal change to support both survival and reproduction rules. Added candidate set so dead cells with 3 live neighbors are considered. Uses `isAlive` helper to distinguish the survival-with-2 case from reproduction.
